description: The introduction of containerization at the Port of London in the mid-1960s drastically reduced the need for manual dock labor. The 1966 strike by dock workers sought to block the automation shift, but ultimately failed to stop the industry transition.

key_events.description: Containerization reaches critical capacity at Tilbury Docks.
National union leaders negotiate severance packages, bypassing rank-and-file strikers.
Shipping companies bypass London for Felixstowe, isolating the strikers.

calibration_notes: Illustrates how structural economic shifts (logistics efficiency) can defeat organized labor despite initial public sympathy.
